{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏_(古代氏族)|lt=Nasu clan|lt_ja=那須氏}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi-no-Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}<br/>(Descendant of {{ill|Ōbiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first imperial {{ill|prince|ja|皇子}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} (the 8th {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}}))
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}}
|出身地 = Nasu-gō, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}
|根拠地 = Nasu-gō, {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}
|人物 = {{ill|Nasu-no-Itate|ja|那須韋提}}
|支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏}} ({{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}})<br/>{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏}}<br/>({{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}} ({{ill|samurai|ja|武家}})<ref>Usually regarded as a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}}; the theory that they descend from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is merely one view.<br/>See also [[#See also]].</ref>)<br/>and others
}}
'''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''nasu no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''nasu kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the region that later became the northeastern part of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}.

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
*According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}}), '''{{ill|Ōomi-no-Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''' — grandson of {{ill|Takenunakawa-no-Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (son of '''{{ill|Ōbiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}''', imperial {{ill|prince|ja|皇子}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} (the 8th emperor)) — was appointed the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏_(古代氏族)|lt=Nasu clan|lt_ja=那須氏}}''' (''nasu-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}). Considered of the same ancestry as the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}, though some say they later became related by marriage.

A clan bearing the same name existed from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} onward: the {{ill|samurai|ja|武家}} {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, known for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}} who shot down the fan target at the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}; there is a theory that they were descendants of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko's Nasu clan (see [[#See also]]).

A dissenting theory holds that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko were of the same lineage as the {{ill|Shimotsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}<ref>See the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}} article.</ref>{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}. This is based on an interpretation of the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}} (see [[#Tutelary Shrine]] below), which reads "respectfully, the deceased lord<ref group="注">{{ill|Nasu-no-Itate|ja|那須韋提}}. See [[#People]].</ref> was the noble descendant of the Hiro clan" — where "Hiro clan" is interpreted as an abbreviation of "{{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}}"<ref group="注">{{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|公 (カバネ)|lt=Kimi|lt_ja=公}} (see {{ill|Mahito|ja|真人}}). The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' records them as descendants of '''{{ill|Toyoki-iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}''' (imperial prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} (the 10th {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}}))'s fourth-generation descendant '''{{ill|Ō-aratawake-no-Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}'''.</ref> (the Shimotsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko were, like the Hirokitsu clan, descendants of {{ill|Arata-wake|ja|荒田別}}).

== Base ==
The base of the kuni no miyatsuko was the later '''Nasu-gō''' of {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡}} in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}<ref name="chizu">''{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Primitive and Ancient Volume, Vol. 2|ja|日本歴史地図|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chizu|lt_ja=日本歴史地図}}''.</ref>. The basin of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川|lt=Naka River|lt_ja=那珂川}} in {{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡}}<ref name="chizu"/>, i.e., most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|lt=Ōtawara City|lt_ja=大田原市}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. The site believed to be the district government office (''gunga'') of Nasu District is the '''{{ill|Nasu-Kanga Site|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', located in Ogawa of {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|lt=Nakagawa Town|lt_ja=那珂川町}} (adjacent to Ōtawara City) in Nasu District, and designated as a national {{ill|historic site|ja|史跡}}.

Municipalities bearing the name "Nasu" include the former {{ill|Nasu Village|ja|那須村}} and the present {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|ja|那須町|lt=Nasu Town|lt_ja=那須町}}.

=== Territory ===
The territory of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was the region then called '''{{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}'''. Nasu Province corresponded to the northern part of the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}} of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} (the whole of '''{{ill|Nasu District|ja|那須郡}}'''. In the ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}'', the "Kenu River," mentioned as a district boundary, is considered to refer to the {{ill|Kinu River|ja|鬼怒川}} (formerly called "Kenu River") and its tributary the {{ill|Kokai River|ja|小貝川}}, expressing the "boundary between {{ill|Kenu|ja|毛野}} and Nasu"<ref>''{{ill|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten: Gunma Prefecture|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|lt_ja=角川日本地名大辞典}}'' ({{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店}}), Kenu-no-kuni entry.</ref>), corresponding to the area around present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|lt=Ōtawara City|lt_ja=大田原市}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263248.html Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (Shimotsuke)] - Nihon Jiten (accessed February 25, 2018 3:10 AM {{ill|Japan Standard Time|ja|日本標準時|lt=JST|lt_ja=日本標準時}})</ref>.

For the etymology of the place name of present-day {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|ja|那須町|lt=Nasu Town|lt_ja=那須町}} in Nasu District of Tochigi Prefecture, which bears the name Nasu District that inherited the name of Nasu Province, see the town's official website: [http://www.town.nasu.lg.jp/0226/info-0000000528-1.html Etymology].

Nasu Province was absorbed into Shimotsukenu Province, which was ruled by the {{ill|Shimotsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, in [[689]] (end of the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}) when the Nasu-no-Itate mentioned [[#People|below]] was appointed {{ill|hyōtoku|ja|評督}} of Nasu-hyō (District). When the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} was enacted in [[701]] ({{ill|Taihō (era)|ja|大宝 (日本)|lt=Taihō|lt_ja=大宝}} 1) (or in the 7th century), Shimotsukenu Province became a {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}}. Shimotsukenu Province was renamed {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}} in [[713]] ({{ill|Wadō|ja|和銅}} 6).

== Tutelary Shrine ==
The {{ill|tutelary kami|ja|氏神}} of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. The '''{{ill|Kasaishi Shrine|ja|笠石神社}}''' in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|lt=Ōtawara City|lt_ja=大田原市}} (formerly Nasu District) in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}} has the {{ill|national treasure|ja|国宝}} '''{{ill|Nasu-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}''' as its {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. However, the shrine itself was founded in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}, much later than the era of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (roughly the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).

=== Tombs ===
{{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} identified the '''{{ill|Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' (''kamisamuraizuka kofun''; {{Coord|36|48|21.1|N|140|7|29.0|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun}}) in present-day Yuzukami, {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|lt=Ōtawara City|lt_ja=大田原市}} (formerly Nasu District) in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, and the '''{{ill|Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''' (''shimosamuraizuka kofun''; {{Coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun}}) in present-day Saradō of the same city (formerly Nasu District) (both {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun (rectangle)|ja|前方後方墳}}) as the tombs of Nasu-no-Atai-Ashite ({{ill|Nasu-no-Itate|ja|那須韋提|lt=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt_ja=那須国造}}. See [[#People]].) and his son Ishimaro, as inscribed on the Nasu-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Monument, and conducted {{ill|excavations|ja|発掘調査}}; however, no {{ill|epitaph|ja|墓誌}} confirming the association was unearthed. Archaeologically, both kofun are estimated to have been built in the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, much earlier than the date of the Nasu-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Monument (the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}).

== People ==
The following are notable figures who served as Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
* '''{{ill|Nasu-no-Itate|ja|那須韋提}}'''
*: A {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Monument, he became the {{ill|hyōtoku|ja|評督}} of {{ill|Nasu-hyō|ja|那須郡|lt=Nasu-hyō|lt_ja=那須評}} in the 4th month of {{ill|Yongchang (Tang)|ja|永昌 (唐)|lt=Yongchang|lt_ja=永昌}} 1 ({{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} 3, [[689]]).

== Descendants ==
* {{ill|Nasu-no-Akatatsu|ja|那須赤竜}}
*: A {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. {{ill|Daryō|ja|大領}} of {{ill|Shirakawa District|ja|白河郡}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Founding ancestor of the Abe {{ill|Mutsu clan|ja|陸奥氏}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Notes ===
{{reflist|group="注"}}

=== Citations ===
{{reflist|2}}

== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}})
* Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}})
* ''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'' ({{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}})
* ''Shintō Daijiten'' ({{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}})
* {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|editor=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|date=1963|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=[[角川書店]]|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[近藤敏喬]]|title=古代豪族系図集覧|year=1993|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=9,356|chapter=|ref=keizu}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[竹内理三]]等|title=[[日本歴史地図]] 原始・古代編 下|year=1982|publisher=[[柏書房]]|isbn=|page=290|chapter=|ref=chizu}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=新川登亀男 |title=「那須国造碑」と仏教 |journal=日本歴史 |issn=03869164 |publisher=吉川弘文館 |year=1992 |month=sep |volume= |issue=532 |pages=p76-79 |naid=40003067906
}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=東野治之 |title=那須国造碑と律令制(第九八回史学会大会報告記事) |journal=史学雑誌 |issn=0018-2478 |publisher=史学会 |year=2000 |volume=109 |issue=12 |pages=2223 |naid=110002363346 |doi=10.24471/shigaku.109.12_2223_2}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=森田悌, 田村充 |title=那須国造碑の研究 |journal=群馬大学教育学部紀要 人文・社会科学編 |issn=03864294 |publisher=群馬大学教育学部 |year=2005 |volume=54 |pages=1-11 |naid=40006708774}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=小貫隆久 |title=那須国造碑と由緒 |journal=栃木県立文書館研究紀要 |issn=13493310 |publisher=栃木県立文書館 |year=2007 |issue=11 |pages=91-98 |naid=40015522731}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author=吉原啓 |title=近世における「再発見」時の那須国造碑とその周辺 |journal=歴史と文化 |issn=0918-6123 |publisher=栃木県歴史文化研究会 ; 1992- |year=2017 |issue=26 |pages=31-39 |naid=40021278092}}

== See also ==
* [[List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|国造#一覧]]
* {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}} - This {{ill|samurai|ja|武家}} Nasu clan claims its own {{ill|primary surname|ja|本姓|lt=honsei|lt_ja=本姓}} to be the {{ill|Nagaie line|ja|藤原長家|lt=Nagaie line|lt_ja=長家流}} of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, but there is a theory that they are descendants of the ancient Nasu clan (see [[#Clan]]), the clan of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko house{{Sfn|太田|1963}}{{要ページ番号|date=2017年7月}}.

{{DEFAULTSORT:なすのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:下野国]]
[[Category:栃木県の歴史]]
[[Category:阿倍氏|*]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:那須国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
